element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns squad selection and basic positioning
One-shot kills on enemy infantry at game start
Fragile but manageable early; becomes more powerful as game progresses
Resource management - must decide between retrieving safely or risking squad
Necessary for destroying buildings and vehicles; limited ammunition
More durable than infantry; require tactical avoidance
Most powerful but rarest ammunition; crucial for tanks
Heavy firepower requires careful squad positioning and rocket use
Stationary but deadly; requires approach planning
Mobile aerial threat; tests squad coordination
Soldiers slowed and cannot fire while crossing; tactical positioning required
Each environment introduces unique terrain features and vehicles

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing all enemies from current area
Finding supply crates and restocking ammunition
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy vehicles appearing (especially tanks and helicopters)
Ammunition running low on explosive weapons
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating pressure - player squad outgunned but superior at start; enemy strength increases progressively through 24 missions with varied environmental and vehicular threats

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic infantry and machine guns; player squad possesses superior firepower; minimal terrain hazards
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy infantry becomes more powerful; introduction of vehicles (Jeeps, tanks); ammunition scarcity forces strategic weapon choices; terrain obstacles (rivers, quicksand, mines) appear
difficulty_curve.late_game: Heavily outnumbered encounters; advanced vehicles (helicopters); limited ammunition and multiple objective types; varied biomes with unique hazards
notable_spikes: Introduction of missile turrets alongside vehicle threats
Late-mission scenarios with helicopter opposition and resource scarcity

notable_level_patterns.description: Players must split squad into smaller units to occupy separate strategic positions, balancing squad strength against tactical advantage
Limited explosive ammunition creates decision points: retrieve crate safely or shoot it from distance, risking ammunition depletion but gaining squad safety
Jungle, snow, and desert environments introduce unique terrain features (rivers, igloos, quicksand) and vehicles (snowmobiles) that require adapted tactics
Enemy-spawning buildings require coordination to approach and destroy with grenades/rockets while managing ongoing enemy infantry pressure
